nodes.claim: Negative feedback is useful for policy improvement, but historical negative actions can become far-field and exert excessive influence; DRPO controls that transition.
must_avoid: finished external numbers before formal closure
must_include: DRPO attenuation of the far-field component
nodes.reader_question: What is the problem, missing link, theory, method, evidence, and implication in one compact sequence?
required_evidence: external tasks marked TBD until formal closure
nodes.role: Summarize the full paper without fixed-advantage scope language, split-manuscript framing, or unverified numerical claims.
nodes.title: Paper Summary

nodes.claim: Off-policy policy optimization needs both attraction toward successful behavior and suppression of known failures, but the latter must remain dynamically controlled.
must_include: off-policy reuse across offline, replay, recommendation, and post-training settings
negative suppression of known failures and competing modes
Positive-only as a stable but potentially limited reference
central question of preserving useful negative feedback without excessive repulsion
nodes.reader_question: Why is negative feedback a necessary policy-improvement signal rather than disposable noise?
nodes.role: Establish the broad off-policy setting, the complementary roles of positive and negative feedback, and the paper's governing question.
nodes.title: Negative Feedback as a Policy-Improvement Resource

nodes.claim: Repeated reuse makes negative influence learner-relative: as the current policy moves away from a fixed historical action, the same label can become persistent or self-amplifying far-field repulsion.
must_include: fixed historical action reused while learner changes
Gaussian score growth with standardized distance
categorical bounded score with persistent support suppression
nodes.reader_question: How can locally informative negative feedback become dynamically dangerous?
nodes.role: Introduce historical reuse as the temporal mechanism and distinguish Gaussian amplification from categorical persistent suppression.
nodes.title: Historical Reuse Turns Local Feedback into Persistent Repulsion

nodes.claim: Existing controls stabilize off-policy learning, but the causal role of learner-relative remoteness remains unidentified when quality, advantage magnitude, rarity, and distance are entangled.
must_avoid: claiming distance is the only factor
must_include: positive-only filtering, global scaling, clipping, support constraints, rarity controls, and quality filtering
lack of a useful-local to destructive-far-field explanation
realistic confounding among reward, advantage, rarity, and distance
matched controls holding quality and coefficient fixed
source and causal interventions as separate responsibilities
nodes.reader_question: What do existing controls solve, and what identification gap remains?
nodes.role: Acknowledge established stabilization methods, isolate the missing dynamic claim, and motivate matched quality--distance controls.
nodes.title: Existing Controls and the Missing Identification Link

nodes.claim: Repulsive Dynamics unifies stable extrapolation, persistent drift, and instability through the aggregate balance of positive and negative update masses and moments.
must_include: Gaussian unbounded scores versus categorical bounded boundary degeneration
nodes.reader_question: What theoretical structure connects beneficial and harmful negative updates?
required_evidence: Proposition on remoteness and score response
nodes.role: Preview the static score relation, repeated-reuse asymmetry, aggregate equilibrium theorem, and policy-family manifestations.
nodes.title: Repulsive Dynamics Explains Stable Extrapolation and Equilibrium Loss

nodes.claim: DRPO preserves informative local negative feedback while attenuating the learner-relative far-field tail that drives the unstable aggregate regime.
must_avoid: old paper followed by a new algorithm
claim that quality hard filtering is mathematically identical to distance tapering
must_include: negative-term reweighting in the empirical actor field
quality selection and remoteness control as separate axes
nodes.reader_question: How does the method intervene on the mechanism rather than merely discard negative data?
nodes.role: Introduce the method target, local-preservation principle, tail guarantee, and distinction between quality selection and remoteness control.
nodes.title: DRPO Controls the Destabilizing Far-Field Term

nodes.claim: The evidence chain assigns occurrence, controlled source identification, causal transmission, phase testing, and external validity to distinct experiments and reports terminal outcomes with a separated failure taxonomy.
must_include: RQ2 matched source and causal identification
nodes.reader_question: How do the experiments jointly support the paper without conflating controlled mechanisms and external validity?
required_evidence: external protocols marked TBD until formal closure
nodes.role: State the four research questions, environment responsibilities, reporting discipline, and contributions.
nodes.title: Evidence Chain and Contributions

nodes.claim: Prior work shows both that negative updates can be filtered for stability and that failures can carry useful learning signal; the missing object is how the same negative action changes as it becomes remote from the learner.
must_include: evidence that suboptimal behavior can suppress bad modes or improve boundaries
negative feedback as informative rather than merely noisy
nodes.reader_question: What is already known about learning from positive, negative, and suboptimal behavior?
nodes.role: Synthesize filtering and negative-information literatures, state the established fact, and isolate the repeated-remoteness gap.
nodes.title: Learning from Negative or Suboptimal Behavior

nodes.claim: Importance correction, clipping, trust regions, stale-policy controls, and rarity-aware rules regulate mismatch or update scale, but usually treat remoteness as a static signal rather than an endogenous repeated-reuse process.
nodes.reader_question: How does this work differ from established off-policy, stale-policy, and low-probability update controls?
nodes.role: Credit mismatch controls, state what they establish, and isolate the dynamic feedback-loop distinction.
nodes.title: Off-Policy, Stale, and Low-Probability Updates

nodes.claim: Conservative value learning, in-support dynamic programming, behavior regularization, and data selection address major offline-RL failures, while DRPO focuses on the signed actor field and selectively preserves local negative information.
must_include: signed actor field as distinct object
nodes.reader_question: How is DRPO related to conservative and behavior-regularized offline reinforcement learning?
nodes.role: Differentiate value extrapolation, policy support, data selection, and signed-actor dynamics while explaining compatibility.
nodes.title: Robust Offline Policy Learning

nodes.claim: The analysis studies a historical signed actor field whose per-sample negative influence factors into advantage severity and score geometry, while frequency and directional coherence determine aggregation.
must_include: fixed advantage-like signal during an actor step
not assumed to equal exact on-policy gradient
nodes.reader_question: What exact update field is analyzed, what is held fixed during an actor step, and how are positive and negative contributions separated?
nodes.role: Define the historical update distribution, fixed actor-step scope, sign decomposition, influence factorization, aggregation variables, and conclusion boundary.
nodes.title: Signed Actor Update and Influence Factorization

nodes.claim: Learner-relative remoteness is negative log probability under the current policy; it maps to Mahalanobis distance for Gaussian policies and surprisal for categorical policies without implying identical amplification laws.
must_include: shared coordinate but distinct response laws
nodes.reader_question: How is far field defined across continuous, categorical, and sequence policies?
nodes.role: Define remoteness and score response, give policy-family mappings, state their dynamic status, and prevent false continuous--discrete equivalence.
nodes.title: Policy-Relative Far Field

nodes.claim: At a fixed policy, Gaussian mean-score magnitude grows without bound with Mahalanobis remoteness, whereas categorical selected-logit response increases with surprisal but saturates.
must_include: Gaussian lower and upper eigenvalue bounds
static geometry does not imply aggregate divergence
nodes.reader_question: How does learner-relative remoteness determine the strength of a single score contribution before any repeated update is considered?
nodes.role: Define the static geometry, state the Gaussian and categorical proposition, interpret both cases, and prevent a premature divergence claim.
nodes.title: Learner-Relative Remoteness and Static Score Response

nodes.claim: For a convex negative log-likelihood along the update path, repeated negative reuse increases remoteness and cannot decrease score response, while sufficiently small positive reuse decreases both.
must_include: negative reuse ascent in negative log likelihood
positive reuse descent with smoothness condition
convexity in Gaussian mean and categorical natural coordinates
Gaussian unbounded versus categorical persistent consequence
single-sample result does not settle aggregate balance
nodes.reader_question: How does repeatedly reusing one fixed historical action turn static geometry into a feedback process?
nodes.role: State the repeated-reuse theorem, its assumptions, family-specific consequences, and its limit as a single-sample result.
nodes.title: Self-Attenuation and Self-Amplification under Reuse

nodes.claim: In a regular exponential family, positive dominance yields a unique stable finite equilibrium when the signed target is feasible, equality yields persistent drift, and negative dominance admits no stable finite equilibrium.
must_include: positive and negative masses and moments
feasible boundary and no finite equilibrium
nodes.reader_question: When do positive attraction and negative repulsion aggregate into stable extrapolation, drift, or instability?
nodes.role: Derive the aggregate field, state all three regimes, interpret extrapolation, give stability conditions, and connect feasibility to boundary loss.
nodes.title: Aggregate Attraction--Repulsion Equilibria

nodes.claim: Negative dominance produces unbounded Gaussian mean displacement and far-field scores, but categorical policies approach the simplex boundary while each direct-logit score remains bounded.
must_include: task collapse and NaN/Inf not implied
nodes.reader_question: What does aggregate instability look like in Gaussian and categorical policies, and which failure labels are justified?
nodes.role: Derive family-specific runaway, distinguish gradient explosion from bounded-gradient boundary degeneration, and preserve reporting boundaries.
nodes.title: Policy-Family Manifestations of Negative Dominance

nodes.claim: The theory predicts a sequence from a Positive-only platform through stable controlled extrapolation to boundary approach or persistent runaway, with targeted far-field attenuation restoring a finite terminal regime when that path is causal.
must_include: boundary approach under stronger or more outward negative moments
persistent drift or non-vanishing residual beyond feasibility
separate task, support-boundary, and numerical events
nodes.reader_question: Which observable outcomes would support or contradict the theory?
nodes.role: Translate the theory into falsifiable predictions, intervention logic, and a separated failure taxonomy.
nodes.title: Observable Regimes and Experimental Predictions

nodes.claim: DRPO controls the signed actor-update distribution by exponentially attenuating learner-relative remote negative updates while leaving positive updates unchanged.
must_avoid: optional quality weights in the main update unless separately registered
claiming quality filtering and remoteness tapering are identical
must_include: quality and remoteness are distinct analysis axes
nodes.reader_question: What is the current paper's self-contained DRPO update?
nodes.role: Define the main method as one current formulation, without old-versus-new chronology or an unregistered combined quality-weight objective.
nodes.title: Distributional Reweighting of Signed Actor Updates

nodes.claim: DRPO replaces the same aggregate negative moment that moves the equilibrium, allowing the method to preserve local displacement while reducing boundary-driving far-field mass.
nodes.reader_question: Which exact theorem object does DRPO modify?
nodes.role: Create an unbroken theory--method equation chain.
nodes.title: Direct Bridge to Theorem 1

nodes.claim: An exponential envelope dominates any finite-order score growth, so the weighted negative gradient vanishes in the far field.
must_include: exponential times polynomial tends to zero
no assumption that sample utility decays exponentially
nodes.reader_question: Why use the exponential form rather than an arbitrary taper?
nodes.role: Provide the method-level tail guarantee without inventing a utility-decay law.
nodes.title: Proposition 2: Vanishing Weighted Far-Field Gradient

nodes.claim: Positive-only, global scaling, hard thresholds, linear tapers, and exponential DRPO isolate which benefits come from retaining local negatives, selecting distance, and controlling total gradient budget.
nodes.reader_question: How will the method be tested against simpler controls?
nodes.role: Define comparisons without predeclaring a winner or conflating quality and distance thresholds.
nodes.title: Controls and Ablations

nodes.claim: C-U1 and D-U1 provide controlled identification, while Hopper/D4RL and Countdown provide external validity; no environment substitutes for another's scientific responsibility.
nodes.reader_question: Why are these environments sufficient and what does each one prove?
nodes.role: Introduce environment construction before reporting results so the claims cannot appear manufactured by an opaque simulator.
nodes.title: Environments and Evidence Roles

nodes.claim: Hopper and Countdown test whether far-field or rare negative influence becomes disproportionately large before task degradation in realistic policy learning.
nodes.reader_question: Does the phenomenon occur outside controlled environments?
nodes.role: Use external evidence as a reality anchor without treating it as the causal isolation experiment.
nodes.title: RQ1: External Occurrence

nodes.claim: When quality, advantage, semantics, count, coefficient, and policy stage are matched, learner-relative distance or rarity independently changes negative influence.
nodes.reader_question: Are far or rare gradients larger because the samples are farther, or merely because they are worse?
nodes.role: Close the paper's decisive rival explanation with a transparent matched protocol.
nodes.title: RQ2a: Matched Badness--Distance and Badness--Rarity Isolation

nodes.claim: Near/far and common/rare interventions test whether the anomalous remote component is the pathway that transmits negative influence into drift, boundary events, and task collapse.
nodes.reader_question: Do large far-field updates actually cause the observed instability?
nodes.role: Move from source identification to causal intervention with equal-budget controls.
nodes.title: RQ2b: Targeted Causal Transmission

nodes.claim: Strength sweeps map the regimes of Theorem 1, while DRPO and matched controls test whether selective far-field attenuation preserves stable extrapolation.
must_include: Positive-only to stable extrapolation to boundary/drift
nodes.reader_question: When does negative feedback become destructive, and can DRPO control that transition?
nodes.role: Unify theorem validation, aggregate-term measurement, and method comparison in one result section.
nodes.title: RQ3: Phase Transition and DRPO Control

nodes.claim: External D4RL and Countdown evaluations test whether controlling remote negative influence improves actual task performance without sacrificing the useful negative signal.
must_include: Countdown model scales and common data bank
nodes.reader_question: Does the mechanism-targeted control matter on public and sequence tasks?
nodes.role: Close the external--controlled--external evidence chain.
nodes.title: RQ4: External Task Closure

nodes.claim: The transferable lesson is to control learner-relative far-field negative influence rather than classify all negative feedback as harmful.
must_include: historical reuse changes its relevance and magnitude
control object is remote negative influence
nodes.reader_question: What should readers remember beyond the specific method?
nodes.role: State the positive principle rather than a list of disclaimers.
nodes.title: Negative Feedback Is a Resource with a Dynamical Failure Mode

nodes.claim: Gaussian and categorical policies share aggregate boundary dynamics while differing in local score growth and failure manifestation.
must_include: Gaussian distance-amplified score and support dynamics
nodes.reader_question: What is genuinely unified across policy families?
nodes.role: Leave one accurate cross-family synthesis.
nodes.title: Continuous and Categorical Synthesis

nodes.claim: DRPO preserves useful negative feedback by attenuating the remote component of the aggregate term that drives equilibrium loss.
nodes.reader_question: What is the final three-sentence contribution?
nodes.role: Close with theory, identification, and method consequence.
nodes.title: Conclusion

nodes.claim: The equilibrium theorem follows from the diffeomorphism between natural and mean parameters in a regular minimal exponential family.
nodes.reader_question: What are the exact existence, uniqueness, boundary, and stability arguments?
nodes.role: Provide the full proof outside the main narrative.
nodes.title: Proof of Theorem 1

nodes.claim: Far-field negative advantages repel the mean and tend to contract variance when standardized distance exceeds one.
nodes.reader_question: What corrects the earlier mean-and-variance expansion account?
nodes.role: Record the exact score signs and joint equilibrium condition.
nodes.title: Corrected Gaussian Mean and Variance Dynamics

nodes.claim: Repeated negative updates produce persistent log-odds decay even though the direct-logit score norm is bounded.
nodes.reader_question: Why is categorical instability not an unbounded Euclidean gradient explosion?
nodes.role: Derive the probability-boundary mechanism accurately.
nodes.title: Categorical Log-Odds and Boundary Behavior

nodes.claim: The controlled environments expose ground truth and permit matched isolation without claiming that construction alone establishes external validity.
nodes.reader_question: How exactly are the controlled environments generated?
nodes.role: Provide enough detail for reproducibility and reviewer inspection.
nodes.title: C-U1 and D-U1 Construction

nodes.claim: All dynamic and ranking claims require fixed protocol ownership, paired seeds, explicit budgets, and terminal audits.
nodes.reader_question: How are false convergence and unfair comparisons prevented?
nodes.role: Centralize experiment governance details.
nodes.title: Stopping, Budget Matching, and Terminal Classification

nodes.claim: Appendix results preserve uncertainty, failed runs, and non-ranking outcomes instead of presenting only favorable endpoints.
nodes.reader_question: What evidence is needed beyond the main figures?
nodes.role: Provide placeholders for complete result deposition.
nodes.title: Additional Tables, Curves, and Negative Results

nodes.claim: Every formal claim is bound to code, configuration, seeds, raw outputs, terminal audit, and a source commit.
nodes.reader_question: How can the study be reproduced and audited?
nodes.role: Connect the paper to repository provenance without embedding live status in the outline.
nodes.title: Code, Data, and Artifact Provenance

nodes.claim: The Optimistic-DRO subdistribution problem yields a quality-based hard-selection solution; this result is part of the current manuscript but is mathematically distinct from learner-relative remoteness tapering.
must_avoid: claim that quality hard filtering derives exponential remoteness tapering
must_include: no implication that hard quality filtering equals exponential distance tapering
nodes.reader_question: What does the Optimistic-DRO result establish in the current paper, and what does it not establish?
required_evidence: explicit separation of quality and remoteness axes
nodes.role: Integrate the valid distributional-selection derivation into the current manuscript while keeping quality selection mathematically distinct from learner-relative remoteness tapering.
nodes.title: Optimistic-DRO Quality Selection and Its Distinct Role

nodes.claim: The manuscript consistently uses corrected Gaussian scale dynamics, the Jacobian of the actual signed field, held-out-context terminology for C-U1, and separate reporting of task, boundary, and numerical failures.
must_include: Gaussian mean repulsion with location-dependent scale dynamics
actual signed-field Jacobian rather than expected Fisher as the stability object
held-out-context or unseen-state generalization for C-U1
separate task collapse, policy-family boundary, and NaN/Inf fields
terminal audit before convergence or long-run language
nodes.reader_question: Which technical conventions must remain consistent across the main text and appendix?
nodes.role: Collect the technical conventions that prevent contradictory statements across theory, experiments, and reporting.
nodes.title: Additional Theoretical and Reporting Clarifications
